samples.xml_description:
Implement an unsigned 8bit multiplier based on pipelining processing. Clock signal used for synchronous operation. Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for chip reset and 1 for reset signal inactive. Input enable signal that controls the multiplication operation. Input signal representing the multiplicand with a data width of 8 bits. Input signal representing the multiplier with a data width of 8 bits. Output enable signal indicating if the multiplication operation is enabled. Product output signal with a data width of 16 bits. 8 16 A register to store the input enable signal mul_en_in. It has a width of 2 bits. A register to store the input multiplicand mul_a. A register to store the input multiplier mul_b. A register to store the sum of partial products. It has a width of 2N bits. A register to store the output product value. It has a width of 2N bits. A register to store the selected bits from the multiplicand based on the multiplier bits.

mul_en_out is the output enable signal that indicates if the multiplication operation is enabled.

mul_en_out_reg is a register that stores the input enable signal mul_en_in.

mul_a_reg is a register that stores the input multiplicand mul_a.

mul_b_reg is a register that stores the input multiplier mul_b.

sum is a register that stores the sum of partial products.

mul_out_reg is a register to store the output product value.

temp is a register that stores the selected bits from the multiplicand based on the multiplier bits.

Implement mul_en_out as the most significant bit of mul_en_out_reg.

Implement a 8-bit register file with 8-bit input and output.

Implement the input control logic with sequential logic.

Implement the input control logic with combinational logic.

Implement the partial product generation logic with combinational logic.

Implement the partial sum calculation logic with sequential logic.

Implement the final product calculation logic with sequential logic.

Implement the output logic with conditional assignment.
